_description: Preterm neonate (age=0) with respiratory distress syndrome. Admitted to public hospital ICU with ventilator support. Tests NEONATAL_ESCALATION_RISK flag (Phase 8), public hospital document relaxation (Phase 9), and per-day ICU-vent stratification (Phase 3).

clinical.chief_complaints: Preterm delivery at 32 weeks, respiratory distress since birth, nasal flaring, intercostal retractions, cyanosis
clinical.history_of_present_illness: Baby born today at 32 weeks of gestation (preterm) by emergency LSCS for foetal distress. Birth weight 1.6 kg. Apgar score 5 at 1 minute, 7 at 5 minutes. Developed respiratory distress immediately after birth. Currently on mechanical ventilation in NICU. Surfactant therapy given 2 hours ago.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Preterm respiratory distress syndrome (RDS) — hyaline membrane disease

examination_findings.general: Preterm neonate, 32 weeks gestation, 1.6 kg, on mechanical ventilation, active, colour improving post surfactant
examination_findings.cvs: Heart sounds normal, no murmur, capillary refill 3 seconds
examination_findings.rs: On MV, FiO2 50%, PEEP 5, PIP 22. Bilateral air entry present post surfactant. Mild subcostal retractions.
examination_findings.abdomen: Soft, liver 2cm BCM, no splenomegaly, umbilical lines in situ
examination_findings.cns: Active, fontanelle flat, no seizures noted

investigations.result_summary: Chest X-ray: Bilateral ground-glass haziness with air-bronchograms. Reticulogranular pattern. Features consistent with hyaline membrane disease (RDS grade III).
ABG: pH 7.28, PaO2 48 mmHg, PaCO2 52 mmHg, HCO3 22 mEq/L, BE -4 (respiratory acidosis). Blood glucose: 48 mg/dL (low). CBC: WBC 12400, Hb 16.2 g/dL.

clinical.family_history: Mother 28 years, G2P1L1. No antenatal complications noted except preterm labour.
clinical.notes: Neonate on MV in NICU. Mother's admission for breastfeeding/KMC should be arranged as per MN package guidelines. Enhancement requests will be needed from Day 2. Public hospital — relaxed doc protocol applies.
